# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that builds Docker images with user-provided image tags and registry URLs. Accept these as build parameters and use them directly in docker build and push commands. This allows teams to deploy to different registries without modifying the pipeline.

Prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent any

    parameters {
        string(
            name: 'IMAGE_TAG',
            defaultValue: 'latest',
            description: 'Docker image tag'
        )
        string(
            name: 'REGISTRY_URL',
            defaultValue: 'registry.example.com',
            description: 'Docker registry URL'
        )
        string(
            name: 'IMAGE_NAME',
            defaultValue: 'myapp',
            description: 'Docker image name'
        )
        string(
            name: 'DOCKERFILE_PATH',
            defaultValue: '.',
            description: 'Path to Dockerfile context'
        )
        credentials(
            name: 'REGISTRY_CREDENTIALS',
            defaultValue: '',
            description: 'Docker registry credentials',
            credentialType: 'com.cloudbees.plugins.credentials.impl.UsernamePasswordCredentialsImpl',
            required: true
        )
    }

    environment {
        SANITIZED_TAG = ''
        SANITIZED_REGISTRY = ''
        SANITIZED_IMAGE_NAME = ''
        SANITIZED_DOCKERFILE_PATH = ''
    }

    stages {
        stage('Validate and Sanitize Inputs') {
            steps {
                script {
                    // Validate IMAGE_TAG: allow only alphanumeric, dots, hyphens, underscores
                    if (!(params.IMAGE_TAG ==~ /^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]{1,128}$/)) {
                        error("Invalid IMAGE_TAG: '${params.IMAGE_TAG}'. Only alphanumeric characters, dots, hyphens, and underscores are allowed (max 128 chars).")
                    }

                    // Validate REGISTRY_URL: allow only valid hostname/IP with optional port
                    // Prevent injection by disallowing shell metacharacters
                    if (!(params.REGISTRY_URL ==~ /^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9._-]{0,253}(:[0-9]{1,5})?(\/[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+)*$/)) {
                        error("Invalid REGISTRY_URL: '${params.REGISTRY_URL}'. Must be a valid registry hostname with optional port and path.")
                    }

                    // Validate IMAGE_NAME: allow only alphanumeric, dots, hyphens, underscores, slashes
                    if (!(params.IMAGE_NAME ==~ /^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9._\/-]{0,255}$/)) {
                        error("Invalid IMAGE_NAME: '${params.IMAGE_NAME}'. Only alphanumeric characters, dots, hyphens, underscores, and slashes are allowed.")
                    }

                    // Validate DOCKERFILE_PATH: prevent directory traversal and command injection
                    if (!(params.DOCKERFILE_PATH ==~ /^[a-zA-Z0-9._\/-]{1,255}$/)) {
                        error("Invalid DOCKERFILE_PATH: '${params.DOCKERFILE_PATH}'. Only alphanumeric characters, dots, hyphens, underscores, and slashes are allowed.")
                    }

                    // Prevent directory traversal
                    if (params.DOCKERFILE_PATH.contains('..')) {
                        error("Invalid DOCKERFILE_PATH: Directory traversal ('..') is not allowed.")
                    }

                    // Whitelist allowed registries to prevent pushing to unauthorized registries
                    def allowedRegistries = [
                        'registry.example.com',
                        'gcr.io/my-project',
                        'docker.io/myorg',
                        '123456789.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com'
                    ]

                    def registryAllowed = allowedRegistries.any { allowed ->
                        params.REGISTRY_URL.startsWith(allowed)
                    }

                    if (!registryAllowed) {
                        error("Registry '${params.REGISTRY_URL}' is not in the allowed registries list: ${allowedRegistries}")
                    }

                    // Set sanitized environment variables
                    env.SANITIZED_TAG = params.IMAGE_TAG
                    env.SANITIZED_REGISTRY = params.REGISTRY_URL
                    env.SANITIZED_IMAGE_NAME = params.IMAGE_NAME
                    env.SANITIZED_DOCKERFILE_PATH = params.DOCKERFILE_PATH

                    echo "Validated inputs:"
                    echo "  Registry: ${env.SANITIZED_REGISTRY}"
                    echo "  Image: ${env.SANITIZED_IMAGE_NAME}"
                    echo "  Tag: ${env.SANITIZED_TAG}"
                    echo "  Dockerfile Path: ${env.SANITIZED_DOCKERFILE_PATH}"
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Checkout') {
            steps {
                checkout scm
            }
        }

        stage('Verify Dockerfile Exists') {
            steps {
                script {
                    def dockerfilePath = "${env.SANITIZED_DOCKERFILE_PATH}/Dockerfile"
                    if (!fileExists(dockerfilePath)) {
                        // Also check if the path itself is a Dockerfile
                        if (!fileExists(env.SANITIZED_DOCKERFILE_PATH)) {
                            error("Dockerfile not found at '${dockerfilePath}' or '${env.SANITIZED_DOCKERFILE_PATH}'")
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Build Docker Image') {
            steps {
                script {
                    def fullImageName = "${env.SANITIZED_REGISTRY}/${env.SANITIZED_IMAGE_NAME}:${env.SANITIZED_TAG}"
                    echo "Building Docker image: ${fullImageName}"

                    // Use Docker Pipeline plugin to avoid shell injection
                    docker.build(
                        fullImageName,
                        "--no-cache --pull ${env.SANITIZED_DOCKERFILE_PATH}"
                    )
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Scan Docker Image') {
            steps {
                script {
                    def fullImageName = "${env.SANITIZED_REGISTRY}/${env.SANITIZED_IMAGE_NAME}:${env.SANITIZED_TAG}"
                    echo "Scanning Docker image for vulnerabilities: ${fullImageName}"

                    // Use Trivy or similar scanner if available
                    // Using Docker plugin methods to avoid shell injection
                    try {
                        def scanResult = sh(
                            script: """
                                trivy image \
                                    --exit-code 1 \
                                    --severity HIGH,CRITICAL \
                                    --no-progress \
                                    '${fullImageName}'
                            """,
                            returnStatus: true
                        )
                        if (scanResult != 0) {
                            unstable("Vulnerability scan found HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerabilities")
                        }
                    } catch (Exception e) {
                        echo "Image scanning tool not available, skipping scan: ${e.message}"
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Push Docker Image') {
            steps {
                script {
                    def fullImageName = "${env.SANITIZED_REGISTRY}/${env.SANITIZED_IMAGE_NAME}:${env.SANITIZED_TAG}"
                    echo "Pushing Docker image: ${fullImageName}"

                    // Use Docker Pipeline plugin with credentials binding
                    // This avoids exposing credentials in shell commands
                    docker.withRegistry("https://${env.SANITIZED_REGISTRY}", params.REGISTRY_CREDENTIALS) {
                        def image = docker.image(fullImageName)
                        image.push()

                        // Also push as 'latest' if the tag is not already 'latest'
                        if (env.SANITIZED_TAG != 'latest') {
                            image.push('latest')
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

    post {
        always {
            script {
                // Clean up local Docker images to save disk space
                def fullImageName = "${env.SANITIZED_REGISTRY}/${env.SANITIZED_IMAGE_NAME}:${env.SANITIZED_TAG}"
                try {
                    sh "docker rmi '${fullImageName}' || true"
                    if (env.SANITIZED_TAG != 'latest') {
                        sh "docker rmi '${env.SANITIZED_REGISTRY}/${env.SANITIZED_IMAGE_NAME}:latest' || true"
                    }
                } catch (Exception e) {
                    echo "Cleanup warning: ${e.message}"
                }
            }
            cleanWs()
        }
        success {
            echo "Docker image ${env.SANITIZED_REGISTRY}/${env.SANITIZED_IMAGE_NAME}:${env.SANITIZED_TAG} built and pushed successfully."
        }
        failure {
            echo "Pipeline failed. Please check the logs for details."
        }
    }
}
